files.00_Overview.md: Severe: Lacks Learning Objectives, Key Takeaways, and proper 3W Framework. Add structured learning outcomes, prerequisite knowledge section, and clear What/Why/How for each concept. Thai-English mixing is inconsistent - standardize to primarily Thai with English technical terms.
files.01_Reacting_Flow_Fundamentals.md: Critical: Missing Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways. Species transport equation (lines 22-27) is redundant with 00_Overview. Add 3W structure: What is reacting flow fundamentals, Why they matter for combustion, How OpenFOAM implements them. Image placeholder (IMG_06_003) needs proper integration.
files.02_Species_Transport.md: Good foundation but needs: 1) Learning Objectives at start, 2) Key Takeaways summary, 3) Simplify initial sections (lines 1-73 too verbose), 4) Practical workflow section (lines 267-366) should be separate file or moved to 06_Practical_Workflow.md. Redundant diffusion model explanations span lines 76-126 unnecessarily.
files.03_Chemistry_Models.md: Well-structured but: 1) Add Learning Objectives, 2) Stiffness explanation (lines 18-52) over-explains with analogy - tighten, 3) Class hierarchy (lines 92-111) redundant if covered elsewhere, 4) Operator splitting algorithm (lines 144-161) duplicates 02_Species_Transport content, 5) Performance optimization (lines 300-323) could be separate advanced topics file.
files.04_Combustion_Models.md: Critical: Extremely shallow coverage. Each model (lines 24-83) gets only 7-15 lines with minimal theory. Expand each model with: physical principles, mathematical formulation, when to use, limitations. Add Learning Objectives. Model comparison table (lines 15-21) lacks quantitative criteria. Selection guide (lines 88-95) too simplistic.
files.05_Chemkin_Parsing.md: Adequate but needs: 1) Learning Objectives, 2) Key Takeaways, 3) Expand file format details (lines 13-20 minimal), 4) Add common pitfalls/troubleshooting, 5) Include example chem.inp snippet with detailed annotation, 6) Conversion command (line 26) needs error handling examples.
files.06_Practical_Workflow.md: Good practical focus but: 1) Missing Learning Objectives, 2) Case setup (lines 15-28) lacks explanation of each file's purpose, 3) Species definition (lines 34-44) duplicates 05_Chemkin_Parsing, 4) Add troubleshooting section, 5) Monitoring section (lines 99-108) needs interpretation guidelines, 6) Missing validation/checklist steps.

global_strategy: Inconsistent application of 3W Framework across all files - only 02 and 03 partially implement What/Why/How structure. Major redundancy issues: species transport equations appear in 00, 01, 02, 03; ODE solver configuration in 01 and 03; practical setup scattered across 02, 05, 06. Language mixing is arbitrary - some Thai-heavy (02, 03), others English-heavy (04, 05, 06). Missing standard pedagogical elements: Learning Objectives absent from all files, Key Takeaways only in 03. Recommended approach: 1) Establish template with mandatory Learning Objectives, Key Takeaways, 3W sections, 2) Consolidate redundant theory into 02_Species_Transport and 03_Chemistry_Models, 3) Expand shallow coverage in 04_Combustion_Models with 200-300% more theoretical depth, 4) Standardize language: Thai narrative with English technical terms in code/math, 5) Create separate troubleshooting/advanced topics file to reduce bloat in 02 and 03.
